title: He’s running Boston to support people like him with Type 1 diabetes
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Collin Bennett, 29, from New Braintree, is running the Boston Marathon for Team Beth Israel Lahey Health.
Bennett was diagnosed with Type 1 Diabetes at age 11 and credits Joslin Diabetes Center for his hope and guidance.
He aims to support a cure for Type 1 diabetes and inspire others with his participation in the marathon.

title: Springfield native Ruth E. Carter keeps making Oscars history
author: Lauren Daley
bullets: Ruth E. Carter, Springfield native, becomes the most-nominated Black woman in Oscar history with her fifth nomination.
Carter could make history as the first Black person to win three Oscars if she wins at the 98th Annual Academy Awards.
Carter designs her own Oscar gown and sources costumes for her films from locations like Brimfield Marketplace in Massachusetts.

title: Brookline schools would see more than 200 layoffs if override fails, officials say
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Brookline schools face over 200 job cuts if a proposed property-tax override is not approved by voters.
Officials state $18.5 million is needed over three years to maintain Brookline's current school staffing and programs.
Brookline voters will decide on the proposed property-tax override at the May 5 annual town election.

title: What to know about the PETS Act aimed at protecting dogs and cats in Mass.
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: The PETS Act would ban the sale of dogs and cats in Massachusetts pet shops to curb puppy mill sales.
The bill aims to expand pet ownership by removing housing and insurance barriers based on a pet's breed.
Massachusetts Legislature is considering the PETS Act, which needs approval from both chambers to become law.

title: As snow melts, drought still a big issue for Mass.
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Massachusetts faces intensified drought despite heavy snowfall, with Central and Northeast regions at Level 3 - Critical Drought.
Officials urge water conservation as limited snowmelt fails to replenish groundwater, impacting rivers and aquifers since 2024.
Massachusetts Water Resources Authority system is unaffected, but sustained rain is needed statewide to improve drought conditions.

title: Why some women are hiding their boyfriends online
author: Meredith Goldstein
bullets: Chanté Joseph's viral essay examines why some women are concealing their boyfriends on social media.
The trend highlights questions about prioritizing male partners in women's public and personal identities.
Chanté's essay spurred debate and artistic reactions, influencing discussions on modern heterosexual relationships.

title: He’s running the Boston Marathon for a camp ‘where illness takes a back seat to fun’
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Griffin O’Neill is running the Boston Marathon to support The Hole in the Wall Gang Camp.
The camp provides a medically safe environment for children with serious illnesses, offering them joy and friendship.
O'Neill's motivation is personal; his fiancée's sister, a former camper, now volunteers as a counselor there.

title: Eagles reportedly ‘will not trade A.J. Brown at this time’ amid Patriots’ continued interest
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: The Philadelphia Eagles are not trading wide receiver A.J. Brown to the New England Patriots at this time.
Trade discussions could resume closer to June 1, when the Eagles gain salary cap relief if a trade occurs post-June 1.
The Patriots reportedly offered a first- and third-round draft pick for Brown, which was initially declined by the Eagles.

title: Jayson Tatum set to return vs. Wizards after injury management off-day
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Jayson Tatum will return for the Celtics against the Wizards after missing a game for injury management.
The Celtics are managing Tatum's right Achilles recovery with a minutes restriction and scheduled off-days.
Baylor Scheierman and Derrick White are probable, while Nikola Vucevic is out for the Wizards game.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: 'War Machine' featuring Alan Ritchson is streaming on Netflix, portraying a soldier with PTSD excelling in Ranger training.
'Zootopia 2', nominated for Oscars, follows Nick and Judy as police partners, now streaming on Disney+.
'Rooster', a comedy starring Steve Carell as a college author, is available for streaming on HBO Max.
